description_long: This object is a classic three-armed candelabrum with a round, sturdy metal base and three upright candle holders, each supporting a partially melted, off-white wax candle. The candles are visibly burning, with small flames flickering atop each wick, and the wax has dripped and pooled irregularly around the candle tops and down the sides. The metal arms curve gracefully outward from a central stem, each ending in a shallow dish to catch melting wax. The base is wide and flat, providing stability, and the metal has a dark, slightly glossy finish, suggesting it is made of wrought iron or a similar material. The candles themselves are uneven in height and shape, indicating they have been used for some time. This candelabrum would be well-suited for decorative or ambient lighting in urban settings such as outdoor cafes, park tables, or historic street corners.
description: A three-armed metal candelabrum with partially melted, burning wax candles and a wide, round base. The object features visible wax drips and a dark, glossy metal finish.
description_view_0: Two candles are prominently visible, with the third partially obscured; wax drips and flames are clearly seen.
description_view_1: All three candles are visible from the front, showing their uneven heights and melted wax details.
description_view_2: The rightmost candle is most prominent, with the other two partially visible; the flames and wax drips are still apparent.
description_view_3: A direct view of the candelabrum's front, with all three candles and their flames clearly visible, highlighting the symmetry of the arms.
